title: Untracked source files are invisible to score and accept
reasoning: Candidate discovery uses `git diff -z --name-only HEAD -- srcDir`, which does not report untracked files. As a result, a candidate that only adds a new source file is treated as a clean worktree, and a candidate that modifies tracked source plus adds untracked source is scored and accepted without measuring or committing the new file. This can produce a misleading keep/reject decision because tests may pass with the untracked file present, while the accepted commit omits it. The accept test verifies that non-source package.json edits stay uncommitted, but it does not cover untracked source files.
recommendation: Include untracked source files in candidate discovery, for example by combining the tracked diff with `git ls-files -z --others --exclude-standard -- srcDir`, then measure and stage the union of source paths.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing accept test mutates a tracked source file and a non-source package.json file, but it never creates an untracked source file that should participate in scoring and acceptance.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a runtime test that creates an initialized repo, writes a new untracked `src/*.ts` file in the scorer worktree, and verifies `score --json` reports it and `accept` commits it when gates pass.
minimumFixScope: Update `changedSource` discovery and the accept/score paths that consume it; add targeted runtime coverage for untracked source additions.

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist, but the implementation has changed to include untracked source discovery. packages/scorer/src/main/scorer.ts now adds untrackedSource to the git plan with git ls-files -z --others --exclude-standard -- srcDir, and changedSource() returns the de-duplicated union of tracked diff paths and untracked source paths. The score and accept paths still consume changedSource(), so both now see untracked source additions; accept stages the resulting source path list with git add -A. Current tests also cover the gap: scorer-runtime.test.ts has untracked-only score coverage for new src/*.ts files and the accept lifecycle test now creates src/new.ts and expects it in the accepted commit while package.json stays uncommitted. pnpm --filter @codenuke/scorer typecheck passed. Targeted Vitest execution was attempted but blocked by the read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write temp files, so runtime tests were not executed in this environment.
commands: pnpm --filter @codenuke/scorer exec vitest run --configLoader runner scorer-runtime.test.ts
